content.body:
Description: Translate the Durak rules into the Colyseus Schema.

Action Items:

Implement the Card, Player, and DurakState schemas we outlined previously.

Build the generateDeck() function, explicitly mapping your custom power values (7=1, ..., A=10).

Build a shuffleAndDeal() function that runs when the room is initialized, giving 5 cards to each connected player and setting the "Huzur" card on the table.

Acceptance Criteria: When a game starts, the state correctly reflects a shuffled deck, a chosen Huzur card, and players with exactly 5 cards in their hand array.
content.title: Implement Game State Schema & Deck Generation

items.title: Implement Game State Schema & Deck Generation

content.body:
Description: We need a minimal, ugly frontend just to prove the frontend can talk to the backend. We will make it pretty later.

Action Items:

Set up a basic frontend project.

Install colyseus.js client SDK.

Write a script to connect to the local Colyseus DurakRoom.

console.log the initial game state (specifically the player's hand and the Huzur) upon connecting.

content.body:
Description: Set up the basic Node.js/TypeScript backend using the Colyseus framework.

Action Items:

Initialize a Node.js project with TypeScript.

Install colyseus, @colyseus/schema, express, and cors.

Create a basic Express server that mounts the Colyseus Server.

Create a blank DurakRoom class and expose the websocket endpoint.

Acceptance Criteria: The server runs locally on a designated port without crashing, and a test client can successfully connect to the empty room.
content.title: Initialize Colyseus Server & Boilerplate

content.body: Closes #6. Adds match-making UI, customizable room settings (max players, private matches, teams mode), dynamic seat assignments, and automatic updates to backend Colyseus configuration.
content.title: Phase 3: Match-making Capabilities & Teams Mode

content.body:
**Background**
To streamline local development, deployment, and testing, we need to containerize the Colyseus server and its supporting database.

**Requirements**
- [ ] Create a Dockerfile for the @durak/server package.
- [ ] Set up a docker-compose.yml to orchestrate the backend and database services (e.g., PostgreSQL/Redis).
- [ ] Ensure environment variables for the database connection are handled correctly.
- [ ] Add instructions to README.md on how to build and run the services using Docker Compose.
content.title: Dockerize backend and database
